subject: torch.divide with rounding_mode='floor'
old: returns NaN when dividing non-zero by zero
new: returns +/-inf when dividing non-zero by zero
truth: torch.divide with rounding_mode='floor' now returns infinity (not NaN) when a non-zero number is divided by zero.
quote: torch.divide with rounding_mode='floor' now returns infinity when a non-zero number is divided by zero

from_fact: returns nan when dividing non-zero by zero
returns +/-inf when dividing non-zero by zero

subject: Legacy tensor constructors and Tensor.new
old: allowed passing both a Tensor and device= as inputs, creating an uninitialized tensor
new: raises an error when passing both a Tensor and device= as inputs
truth: Legacy tensor constructors (e.g. torch.LongTensor) and Tensor.new no longer accept both a Tensor and device= as inputs; use torch.tensor(), torch.as_tensor(), or torch.empty() instead.
quote: Legacy tensor constructors and Tensor.new no longer support passing both Tensor and device as inputs

from_fact: allowed passing both a tensor and device= as inputs, creating an uninitialized tensor
raises an error when passing both a tensor and device= as inputs

subject: torch.Tensor(tensor, device=device) constructor
truth: Stop using torch.Tensor(tensor, device=device); use torch.tensor(...) or torch.as_tensor(...) instead.
quote: RuntimeError: Legacy tensor constructor of the form torch.Tensor(tensor, device=device) is not supported. Use torch.tensor(...) or torch.as_tensor(...) instead.

subject: tensor.new(tensor, device=device) method
truth: Stop using tensor.new(tensor, device=device); use torch.as_tensor(...) instead.
quote: RuntimeError: Legacy tensor new of the form tensor.new(tensor, device=device) is not supported. Use torch.as_tensor(...) instead.

subject: torch.divide rounding_mode='true'
new: torch.divide(a, b, rounding_mode=None)
truth: Stop using rounding_mode='true' in torch.divide; use rounding_mode=None (or omit the argument) instead.
quote: torch.divide's undocumented rounding_mode='true' option has been removed, and instead rounding_mode=None should be passed to indicate no rounding should take place.

subject: import torch.tensor as tensor
truth: Stop using 'import torch.tensor as tensor'; use 'from torch import tensor' instead.
quote: Instead, use `from torch import tensor`

subject: numpy as required dependency in binary release
new: numpy is no longer a required dependency
truth: numpy is no longer a required dependency in binary releases; install it separately if needed.
quote: `numpy` is no longer a required dependency

from_fact: numpy is no longer a required dependency

subject: torch.autograd.gradcheck.get_numerical_jacobian and get_analytical_jacobian
old: supported functions returning complex output and grad_out != 1
new: no longer support complex output or grad_out != 1
truth: Stop calling get_numerical_jacobian/get_analytical_jacobian with functions returning complex output or with grad_out not equal to 1; wrap functions to return real/imaginary component and use grad_out=1.0.
quote: torch.autograd.gradcheck.get_numerical_jacobian and torch.autograd.gradcheck.get_analytical_jacobian no longer support functions that return complex valued output as well as any other values of grad_out not equal to 1

from_fact: supported functions returning complex output and grad_out != 1
no longer support complex output or grad_out != 1

subject: torch.autograd.gradcheck exception type
new: throws GradcheckError
truth: torch.autograd.gradcheck now throws GradcheckError instead of a generic exception.
quote: torch.autograd.gradcheck now throws GradcheckError

subject: gradcheck error type
new: GradcheckError
truth: Stop checking for RuntimeError explicitly; check for torch.autograd.GradcheckError instead when silencing gradcheck errors with raise_exception=False.
quote: All errors that are able to be silenced by raise_exception=False now raise GradcheckError (which inherits from RuntimeError). If you explicitly check that the type of the error is RuntimeError you'll need to update your code to check for GradcheckError instead.

subject: in-place modification of views
new: forbidden (raises error)
truth: Stop modifying in-place views created by custom functions, functions returning multiple views (split/chunk), or in no-grad mode; these now raise an error instead of a deprecation warning.
quote: In-place modification of views will now raise an error if that view was created by a custom function or a function that returns multiple views, or if the view was created in no-grad mode. Modifying in-place a view created in the situations above are error-prone and have been deprecated since v1.5.0. Doing these in-place modifications are now forbidden.

subject: nn.MultiheadAttention bias parameter on out_proj
old: out_proj always had bias even when bias=False
new: out_proj has no bias when bias=False
truth: Stop relying on nn.MultiheadAttention's out_proj having a bias parameter when bias=False; it is now None.
quote: This fix is BC-breaking for the bias=False case as it will now result in no bias parameter for the output projection layer.

from_fact: out_proj always had bias even when bias=false
out_proj has no bias when bias=false

subject: full backward hooks
old: full backward hooks did not fire when no input requires gradients
new: full backward hooks always fire during backward pass regardless of input requires_grad
truth: Full backward hooks now always fire during backward pass, even when no input requires gradients.
quote: Prior to this release, full backward hooks were not fired when no input requires gradients. This has been changed so that full backward hooks will always fire during the backward pass, regardless of whether or not any input requires gradients.

from_fact: full backward hooks did not fire when no input requires gradients
full backward hooks always fire during backward pass regardless of input requires_grad

subject: DataLoader worker NumPy seeding
old: DataLoader workers with fork context had same NumPy random seed across workers and epochs by default
new: DataLoader workers now get independent NumPy random seeds per worker per epoch by default
truth: DataLoader with num_workers > 0 now sets independent NumPy random seeds per worker by default, changing reproducibility of NumPy-based operations in workers.
quote: DataLoader with num_workers > 0 will now set independent random seed for NumPy random functions on each worker by default.

from_fact: dataloader workers with fork context had same numpy random seed across workers and epochs by default
dataloader workers now get independent numpy random seeds per worker per epoch by default

subject: IterableDataset
old: String type annotations on IterableDataset were silently ignored
new: String type annotations are now evaluated and raise Exception if invalid
truth: Stop using string type annotations as workarounds on IterableDataset; they are now evaluated and will raise exceptions for invalid types.
quote: # like using string to  represent the invalid type annotation is not supported now.

from_fact: string type annotations on iterabledataset were silently ignored
string type annotations are now evaluated and raise exception if invalid

subject: IterableDataset.__iter__
old: Return type of __iter__ was not enforced
new: Return type of __iter__ must be an Iterator, otherwise TypeError is raised
truth: Stop returning non-Iterator types from IterableDataset.__iter__; the return type annotation must be Iterator or a TypeError will be raised.
quote: Raises TypeError if the return type of __iter__ is not an Iterator

from_fact: return type of __iter__ was not enforced
return type of __iter__ must be an iterator, otherwise typeerror is raised

subject: IterableDataset.__iter__ return type argument
old: Iterator[X] with X not matching dataset type was allowed
new: Iterator[X] requires X to be a subtype of IterableDataset.type attribute, otherwise TypeError
truth: Stop using Iterator[X] on __iter__ where X is not a subtype of the IterableDataset's type parameter; it will raise TypeError.
quote: A new attribute named `type` has been introduced for `IterableDataset` using the typing annotation at each class declaration.

from_fact: iterator[x] with x not matching dataset type was allowed
iterator[x] requires x to be a subtype of iterabledataset.type attribute, otherwise typeerror

subject: IterableDataset metaclass
old: IterableDataset had no metaclass, user-defined metaclasses worked
new: IterableDataset now has a metaclass, conflicting with user-defined metaclasses
truth: Stop using custom metaclasses on IterableDataset subclasses; the new metaclass will conflict and raise errors.
quote: A new attribute named `type` has been introduced for `IterableDataset` using the typing annotation at each class declaration.

from_fact: iterabledataset had no metaclass, user-defined metaclasses worked
iterabledataset now has a metaclass, conflicting with user-defined metaclasses

subject: torch.Tensor metaclass
new: type(type(torch.tensor([1]))) returns <class 'torch._C._TensorMeta'>
truth: torch.Tensor now has a non-trivial metaclass _TensorMeta, which may cause unanticipated interactions with Python metaclass features.
quote: Tensor now has a non-trivial metaclass. This shouldn't be user observable, as Tensor already inherits from a C defined class (and is thus incompatible with other typical metaclasses), but there may be unanticipated interactions with other language features in Python.

subject: C++ resize_ / resize_as_ / resize_as_sparse_ / sparse_resize_ / sparse_resize_and_clear_
new: Return const Tensor&
truth: Stop assigning the result of resize_, resize_as_, resize_as_sparse_, sparse_resize_, or sparse_resize_and_clear_ to a non-const Tensor&; they now return const Tensor&.
quote: The C++ signature for `resize_`, `resize_as_`, `resize_as_sparse_`, `sparse_resize_`, and `sparse_resize_and_clear_` has changed to return a `const Tensor&` instead of a `Tensor&`.

subject: ATen Reduction Ops and kron_out
old: Undefined tensor passed as out= argument was accepted
new: Undefined tensor passed as out= argument now throws an error
truth: Stop passing undefined tensors as the out argument to ATen reduction ops and kron_out; they now throw an error.
quote: Some ATen Reduction Ops as well as `kron_out` now throw an error when an undefined tensor is passed as input for `out` argument

from_fact: undefined tensor passed as out= argument was accepted
undefined tensor passed as out= argument now throws an error

subject: C++ reduction ops (sum_out, nansum_out, prod_out, std_var_out)
old: allowed passing undefined Tensor as out argument
new: require users to allocate result Tensor before calling these ops
truth: C++ reduction ops sum_out, nansum_out, prod_out, std_var_out now require a pre-allocated result Tensor; passing an undefined Tensor raises c10::Error at runtime.
quote: C++ API for the reductions ops like `sum_out`, `nansum_out`, `prod_out`, `std_var_out` have been changed to require users allocating result Tensor before calling these ops.

from_fact: allowed passing undefined tensor as out argument
require users to allocate result tensor before calling these ops

subject: allocate_reduction_result C++ API
new: resize_reduction_result
truth: Replace allocate_reduction_result with resize_reduction_result; the old function allocated a new result Tensor, the new one only resizes an existing one.
quote: The C++ API `allocate_reduction_result` has changed to `resize_reduction_result` to disallow allocating result Tensor in these reduction ops.

subject: expand_inplace and expand_outplace C++ utility functions
new: return c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor>
truth: expand_inplace and expand_outplace now return c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor> instead of std::tuple<Tensor>; the returned object must not outlive the original Tensor it refers to.
quote: The C++ API utility functions `expand_inplace` and `expand_outplace` now return `c10::MaybeOwned<Tensor>` instead of `std::tuple<Tensor>`

subject: TorchScript recursive scripting of class type module attributes
old: class type module attributes were ignored unless explicitly marked with @torch.jit.script
new: class type module attributes are automatically scripted when the module is scripted
truth: TorchScript now recursively scripts class type module attributes; previously ignored attributes may now cause runtime errors if the class lacks type annotations, as types default to Tensor instead of the actual type.
quote: This change is BC-breaking because it will result in class type module attributes being scripted when a module instance is scripted.

from_fact: class type module attributes were ignored unless explicitly marked with @torch.jit.script
class type module attributes are automatically scripted when the module is scripted

subject: torch.quantization.quantize_fx.convert_fx
new: is_reference argument
truth: Stop using the 'debug' argument in convert_fx; use 'is_reference' instead.
quote: `torch.quantization.quantize_fx.convert_fx`’s `debug` argument has been changed to `is_reference` ([#52179](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/52179)).

subject: torch.cat quantization
new: quantized to torch.cat directly
truth: torch.cat is now quantized to torch.cat instead of torch.ops.quantized.cat; all inputs must share the same qparam.
quote: torch.cat is now quantized to torch.cat instead of torch.ops.quantized.cat

subject: DistributedDataParallel
truth: DistributedDataParallel no longer supports single-process multi-device mode; spawn one process per device instead.
quote: DistributedDataParallel now errors out when users attempt to use it in single-process multi-device mode

subject: DistributedDataParallel
old: device_ids could be a list of multiple device IDs for single-process multi-GPU mode
new: device_ids must be None or contain a single element
truth: Stop passing multiple device IDs to DistributedDataParallel; device_ids must be None or a list with exactly one element.
quote: ValueError: device_ids can only be None or contain a single element.

from_fact: device_ids could be a list of multiple device ids for single-process multi-gpu mode
device_ids must be none or contain a single element

quote: Replaced `torch.distributed.launch` with `torch.distributed.elastic_launch`

truth: Stop using torch.symeig; use torch.linalg.symeig instead.

subject: torch.chain_matmul
new: torch.linalg.chain_matmul
truth: Stop using torch.chain_matmul; use torch.linalg.chain_matmul instead.
quote: `torch.{cholesky, qr, symeig, chain_matmul, solve, eig, matrix_rank, lstsq}` have been deprecated in favor of `torch.linalg.{cholesky, qr, symeig, chain_matmul, solve, eig, matrix_rank, lstsq}`

subject: torch.solve

truth: Stop using torch.solve; use torch.linalg.solve instead.

subject: torch.eig
new: torch.linalg.eig
truth: Stop using torch.eig; use torch.linalg.eig instead.

subject: torch.matrix_rank
new: torch.linalg.matrix_rank
truth: Stop using torch.matrix_rank; use torch.linalg.matrix_rank instead.

subject: torch.lstsq

subject: AutoNonVariableTypeMode (C++)

truth: Stop using at::AutoNonVariableTypeMode; use c10::AutoDispatchBelowAutograd for kernel implementations or c10::InferenceMode for user-facing inference.
quote: If you are looking for a user-facing API to enable running your inference-only workload, please use `c10::InferenceMode`.

subject: Custom autograd Function instantiation
old: Instantiating custom autograd Function then calling .apply
new: Calling .apply() directly on the class (classmethod)
truth: Stop instantiating custom autograd Function objects; call Func.apply() directly as a classmethod instead.
quote: Instantiating a custom autograd function is now deprecated and will raise a warning. Users should call `.apply()` on the class itself because it is a static method.

from_fact: instantiating custom autograd function then calling .apply

subject: torch::linalg::linalg_det and torch::linalg::linalg_norm (C++)
new: torch::linalg::det, torch::linalg::norm
truth: Stop using torch::linalg::linalg_det and torch::linalg::linalg_norm; use torch::linalg::det and torch::linalg::norm instead.

subject: ProcessGroup RPC backend
truth: ProcessGroup RPC backend is deprecated and will be removed after 1.9; use TensorPipe backend instead.
quote: ProcessGroup RPC backend is being deprecated and 1.9 is the last release which will carry it. The default RPC backend is TensorPipe which is the recommended backend to use over ProcessGroup.

subject: get_analytical_jacobian and get_numerical_jacobian
truth: Stop using torch.autograd.gradcheck.get_analytical_jacobian and get_numerical_jacobian; use torch.autograd.gradcheck instead.
quote: `torch.autograd.gradcheck.get_numerical_jacobian` and `torch.autograd.gradcheck.get_analytical_jacobian` no longer support functions that return complex valued output as well as any other values of `grad_out` not equal t

subject: torch.clamp
old: min and max arguments only accepted scalars
new: min and max arguments now accept tensor inputs
truth: torch.clamp now accepts tensor inputs for min and max arguments, which may change dtype promotion or shape broadcasting behaviour compared to passing scalars.
quote: Added support for tensor inputs for `min` and `max` arguments in `torch.clamp`

from_fact: min and max arguments only accepted scalars
min and max arguments now accept tensor inputs

subject: torch.std, torch.var, torch.std_mean, torch.var_mean
new: added correction parameter
truth: torch.std, var, std_mean, var_mean now have a correction argument that changes the divisor from n to n - correction, which may silently change results if code relied on the old default behaviour.
quote: Added overloads for `torch.{std, var, std_mean, var_mean}` with a correction argument specifying the difference between the sample size and number of degrees of freedom.

subject: torch.sort
old: no stable keyword argument on CPU
new: added stable keyword argument on CPU
truth: torch.sort on CPU now has a stable kwarg; the default sort algorithm may change behaviour for equal elements if stable is not explicitly set.
quote: Added support for stable sort algorithm on CPU by a new kwarg `stable`

from_fact: no stable keyword argument on cpu
added stable keyword argument on cpu

subject: torch.quantile

subject: torch.std and torch.var
old: returned complex-valued output for complex inputs
new: return real-valued output tensor for complex inputs
truth: torch.std and torch.var now return a real-valued output tensor for complex inputs instead of complex-valued output.
quote: Added complex support for all reductions for `torch.{std, var}` to return a real valued output tensor for complex inputs

from_fact: returned complex-valued output for complex inputs
return real-valued output tensor for complex inputs

out= input tensor is safely cast and copied

from_fact: cross-device out and input tensors were allowed or silently failed

quote: Modified the order of asserts to correct the error message when nan appears in `torch.multinomial` on CUDA

from_fact: error message for nan was different or unclear
order of asserts changed, error message corrected for nan

subject: PowerSGDState start_powerSGD_iter default

subject: PyObject preservation for tensors
old: Tensors with no Python-side references but still referenced in C++ (e.g., saved for autograd) would get deallocated, and a new Python object would be created next time it passes from C++ to Python.
truth: Stop relying on tensors being deallocated when Python references are dropped but C++ references remain; the PyObject is now preserved as long as any reference exists on either side.
quote: Previously, tensors in python that no longer had any python-side references (but still had references in C++, e.g. if it’s saved for autograd) would get deallocated, and we would create a new Python object to replace it next time it passes from C++ to Python. We now preserve the PyObject as long as there are any references on either the python or C++ side.

>>> buffer = io.BytesIO()
>>> torch.save(a, buffer)
>>> buffer.seek(0)
>>> b = torch.load(buffer)
>>> print(a.foo)
hey
>>> print(b.foo)
AttributeError: 'Tensor' object has no attribute 'foo'

# torch.nn.Parameter behavior
>>> a = nn.Parameter()
>>> a.foo = 'hey'
>>> buffer = io.BytesIO()
>>> torch.save(a, buffer)
>>> buffer.seek(0)
>>> b = torch.load(buffer)
>>> print(a.foo)
